open_positions.name: Extreme Networks
open_positions.thesis: Sympathy selling off CALX print - both are networking/access equipment names. EXTR down 5.8% on 3x volume with no company-specific news in feed, while XLK is up 2.2%, strongly suggests read-through trade. Catalyst: CALX -14% print dragging networking peers. Falsified by: Discovery of EXTR-specific negative catalyst (pre-announce, lost deal, downgrade) would validate the move; continued sector-wide weakness would suggest broader enterprise IT spend concern.
open_positions.latest_reasoning: The sympathy-selling catalyst (CALX -14%) delivered the predicted mean reversion within the 6-day horizon. EXTR rallied 16.7% off capitulation lows and is now approaching fair value relative to sector momentum. Thesis remains intact (no EXTR-specific negative news surfaced), but the motive force is fading—continued gains would require a fresh catalyst (earnings beat, upgrade, broader XLK re-acceleration). We're at day 6 of an estimated 5-10 day mean-reversion window. Risk/reward is now neutral to slightly adverse; holding but monitoring for EXIT trigger on either sustained weakness below $21 or stall above $22.50.

trade_log.why: Opened per flag: LIKELY OVERDONE conf 3 — CALX -14% print dragging networking peers
